findings.title: IKadenaKeypair declared 6 times across signing/ + interactions/*
findings.lines: signing/types.ts:22-30; activateFunctions.ts:27-33; dexFunctions.ts:31; coilFunctions.ts:15-21 (non-exported); kpayFunctions.ts; ouroFunctions.ts (Phase-2b documented exemption)
findings.description: The signing-ready keypair shape has six independent declarations. One canonical (signing/types.ts), one documented Phase-2b backwards-compat (ouroFunctions.ts), and FOUR undocumented duplicates that consumers can import as if they were the same type. The coilFunctions.ts copy is non-exported and invisible to find-references tools.
findings.evidence: signing/types.ts:22-30 has the canonical IKadenaKeypair with seedType including 'foreign' and encryptedSecretKey: unknown. activateFunctions.ts:27-33 has a copy missing 'foreign' seedType and uses encryptedSecretKey: any. coilFunctions.ts:15-21 has another copy that is not even exported. Drift between copies is now a runtime hazard.
findings.impact: TypeScript treats duplicates as structurally compatible today, but the moment any one copy adds a required field (or any consumer narrows on seedType === 'foreign') the drift becomes a runtime/compile error that lands silently on partial migrations.
findings.suggestedFix: Delete local declarations in activateFunctions.ts, dexFunctions.ts, kpayFunctions.ts, coilFunctions.ts. Replace with `import type { IKadenaKeypair } from '../signing/types'`. Keep ouroFunctions.ts copy with @deprecated JSDoc.

findings.title: withFailover is dead code — submit paths bypass it entirely
findings.description: withFailover<T>(fn) wraps a fetch/submit closure with primary→fallback retry on network errors. It is exported but never imported. Every createClient(getPactUrl(...)) site goes through getPactUrl, which returns a string fixed at module init. A primary-node 503 at submit-time fails the whole transaction with no retry on fallback.
findings.evidence: grep 'withFailover' src/ returns 1 hit (the definition itself). All ~30 createClient(getPactUrl(...)).submit(...) call sites in interactions/* directly invoke submit without any retry wrapper.
findings.impact: A primary-node hiccup during submit causes a user-visible 'transaction failed' even though the fallback host is healthy. The library advertises automatic failover but only the read-side host resolution is wired in.
findings.suggestedFix: Wrap every submit(...) and dirtyRead(...) call site with withFailover. Two patterns: per-call wrap, or client factory that returns failover-wrapped methods. Guard submit retry with request-key dedup to avoid double-submit hazard.

findings.title: rawCalibratedDirtyRead bypasses node failover via static PACT_URL default
findings.description: Default Pact URL is the static PACT_URL constant from ../constants, not getActivePactUrl(chainId). Every read that doesn't explicitly pass pactUrl ignores node failover entirely.
findings.evidence: Line 17: `import { KADENA_CHAIN_ID, KADENA_NETWORK, PACT_URL } from '../constants';` Line 49: `const pactUrl = options?.pactUrl ?? PACT_URL;` — getActivePactUrl exists in nodeFailover.ts:91-93 but is not imported here.
findings.impact: Reads continue hitting node1 even after failover switched the active host to node2. Plus no request timeout — a hung node pins a UI tab indefinitely.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with `const pactUrl = options?.pactUrl ?? getActivePactUrl(chainId);`. Add a Promise.race-based timeout (15s default).

findings.title: nodeFailover module-global state leaks across consumers
findings.description: PRIMARY_HOST, FALLBACK_HOST, currentHost, customGasLimit, retryTimer are module-level let bindings. setNodeConfig() mutates them. In multi-tenant SSR or jsdom test runners, one tenant's setNodeConfig flips the active host for every other tenant.
findings.evidence: Lines 25-33 declare module-let bindings. setNodeConfig at 128-151 mutates them. No isolation primitive (class instance, AsyncLocalStorage) wraps the state.
findings.impact: Multi-tenant SSR cross-contamination; test isolation issues; race condition on concurrent setNodeConfig calls leaking retryTimer.
findings.suggestedFix: Quick: export resetNodeFailover() helper for tests. Proper: convert module to a NodeFailover class with a default singleton for backwards-compat.

findings.title: Wallet → interactions backwards layering (KadenaWallet imports from interactions/)
findings.description: wallet/ is the lower-level module; interactions/ is higher-level. KadenaWallet.getBalance() imports from interactions/kadenaFunctions, inverting the dependency direction.
findings.evidence: Line 14: `import { getBalance } from '../interactions/kadenaFunctions';` — this is the sole offender. Today compiles because interactions/kadenaFunctions doesn't reciprocate, but any future cross-import lands an ESM circular.
findings.impact: Anyone consuming @stoachain/ouronet-core/wallet pulls in the entire interactions tree (including @kadena/client, pactRead, every constant), defeating the subpath-only-consumer pattern documented at interactions/index.ts:1-14.
findings.suggestedFix: Either move getBalance into a small network/ helper, accept a balance-resolver callback in KadenaWallet's constructor, or drop getBalance() entirely and make balance fetching the consumer's responsibility.

findings.title: pactRead seam bypassed by ~16 pure-read call sites
findings.description: src/reads/pactReader.ts exists exactly so consumers can plug in a single reader implementation that all interaction code respects. ~16 read sites still call createClient(getPactUrl(...)).dirtyRead(tx) directly, bypassing the seam. (Note: ~21 additional sim-before-submit sites legitimately bypass — they were excluded from this count after validator + integration-checker reconciliation; original architecture-auditor count of 37 was incorrect.)
findings.evidence: addLiquidityFunctions.ts:252-258 builds a Pact transaction and calls createClient(getPactUrl(KADENA_CHAIN_ID)) directly instead of going through pactRead. Similar pattern at 15 other locations.
findings.impact: OuronetUI's cache-aware reader misses these calls — every keystroke re-fetches. Smart Swap flickering reappears. HUB's batch reader cannot collapse them either.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace the 16 sites with pactRead(pactCode, { tier: 'T?' }). Audit PR by grepping for createClient(getPactUrl in src/interactions to ensure no PURE read site is missed.

findings.title: Empty catches fabricate fallback values (1.0 USD, 0% fee, 8 decimals)
findings.description: Three read functions return fabricated values when the chain call fails. Values look plausible but are never marked as fallbacks — callers can't distinguish 'the chain said 8' from 'the read crashed'. Especially dangerous for getStoaPriceUSD because 1.0 is a believable price that could feed into a swap-quote calculation.
findings.evidence: ouroFunctions.ts:2042-2054 — getStoaPriceUSD returns 1.0 on success-without-data, 1.0 on failure status, and 1.0 in the catch. dexFunctions.ts:1308 — getTokenDecimals returns 8 on every failure. dexFunctions.ts:1346-1349 — getPoolTotalFee returns 0 on every failure.
findings.impact: A network failure at the price oracle is indistinguishable from 'the price is 1.0'. A swap quote built on this fabricated 1.0 could under- or over-pay by orders of magnitude.
findings.suggestedFix: Return a discriminated union: Promise<{ ok: true; value: number } | { ok: false; reason: 'network' | 'no-data' }>. Or return null and force caller to handle absence. Log failures via telemetry, not just console.error.

findings.title: No timeout on chain submit / dirtyRead calls
findings.description: @kadena/client's submit and dirtyRead Promises are unbounded. A primary-node hang causes a user-visible spinner that never resolves. Failover doesn't help here because failover triggers on network errors — a slow-but-not-failing response simply hangs.
findings.evidence: rawCalibratedRead.ts:58-59: `const { dirtyRead } = createClient(pactUrl); const response = await dirtyRead(transaction);` — no Promise.race, no AbortController.
findings.impact: UI: spinner pinned indefinitely; user clicks again → double submit. HUB: a single tenant's slow request blocks an event loop slot until GC.
findings.suggestedFix: Add configurable timeout — sensible defaults: 15s for dirtyRead, 60s for submit. Use Promise.race with a setTimeout-based reject.

findings.title: V1 decrypt error swallowing — KDF leak + UX confusion + info disclosure
findings.description: Three concerns reinforce each other: (1) V1's decryptString catch-all collapses every failure into the same string. (2) smartDecrypt double-tries V1 then V2, leaking ~1.5s timing on wrong-password V1 envelope. (3) console.error('Decryption error:', error) at v1.ts:123 leaks underlying crypto error to browser console.
findings.evidence: v1.ts:122-127 wraps the entire decrypt in try/catch and maps every error to 'Failed to decrypt data. Invalid password or corrupted data.' v2.ts:177-187 (smartDecrypt) tries V1 first (~50ms KDF), then on catch retries V2 (~1.5s KDF) — observable timing differential between V1/V2 envelopes on wrong password.
findings.impact: UX: a corrupt envelope reads 'wrong password' — users go in circles. Side channel: wrong-password attempt for V1 envelope takes ~1.5s longer than V2 (observable from a malicious page). Console disclosure: underlying crypto error logged in browser console.
findings.suggestedFix: Distinguish error types (InvalidEnvelopeError vs WrongPasswordError). Drop console.error in production paths. In smartDecrypt, use isEncryptedV2 as the sole discriminator; never attempt V1 KDF on a V2 envelope.

findings.title: CFM-builders use unescaped string interpolation into Pact code
findings.description: Every buildXPactCode builder produces Pact-code by interpolating untrusted-shaped fields directly into the template. Patron, sender, receiver, token-id arrive from UI inputs. None are validated; none are escaped against the embedded `"` quote.
findings.evidence: cfmBuilders.ts:55-57: `return \`(${KADENA_NAMESPACE}.TS01-C1.DPTF|C_Transfer "${p.patron}" "${p.tokenId}" "${p.sender}" "${p.receiver}" ${dec} ${p.method})\`;` — a field containing `"` would close the string early and inject Pact tokens.
findings.impact: Chain rejects malformed Pact code so this isn't a route to an unauthorised tx. But: opaque chain errors instead of UI validation; future builders for richer fields (Pact strings with backslashes, JSON values) inherit the gap; the builder is the public seam where any future caller (HUB-side flows with less-trusted inputs) passes strings through.
findings.suggestedFix: Add a pactString(s: string) helper that asserts s matches the legal account/token-id charset and throws on mismatch. Wrap every interpolated field. Add a quote-injection negative test per builder.

findings.title: Tests missing for pactReader, KadenaWalletBuilder, transactionErrors
findings.description: Three security-sensitive surfaces have zero test coverage: (1) pactReader.ts setPactReader/pactRead injection seam. (2) KadenaWalletBuilder.createWalletPairFromMnemonic BIP39 vs Chainweaver dispatch. (3) transactionErrors classifier driving error UX.
findings.evidence: ls tests/ shows 12 files: cfm-builders, codex-codec, dalos-integration, encryption-upgrade, encryption, gas, guard, network, pact-format, signing, smart-account-auth, strategy. None match the three flagged surfaces.
findings.impact: Future refactor of setPactReader could revert the file to a no-op without any test failing. Mnemonic-dispatch error → user passes Koala 24-word seed, gets Chainweaver-derived (different) keys, loses access. transactionErrors regressions surface to users as garbled error messages.
findings.suggestedFix: Add three test files. Bare-minimum: pact-reader.test.ts (default reader happy path; setPactReader round-trip; reset); wallet-builder.test.ts (koala-24 fixture pubkey; chainweaver-12 different pubkey; mismatched length throws); transaction-errors.test.ts (known error strings classify; unknown falls through).

findings.title: Test coverage gaps — encryption boundary, migration, pact format
findings.description: Existing encryption tests cover happy-path round-trip but not: V2 envelope tampered to V1 shape; smartEncrypt with schemaVersion null vs '0' vs '1'; seedTypeMigration round-trip from a Phase-1 codex; formatDecimalForPact edge cases (scientific notation, leading zeros, locale separators).
findings.evidence: Reading existing tests/ files shows happy-path coverage only. Tampered envelopes, cross-version flows, and migration round-trips are absent.
findings.impact: Regression in any of these paths slips through silently. The codex-export test only verifies happy-path round-trip; tampered envelopes are not exercised.
findings.suggestedFix: Extend tests/encryption.test.ts with cross-version cases. Add tests/seed-type-migration.test.ts. Extend tests/pact-format.test.ts with edge cases.

findings.title: deserializeCodex accepts any object that carries version: '1.2'
findings.description: deserializeCodex validates the version field but does NOT validate the shape of kadenaWallets, ouronetWallets, addressBook, or uiSettings. Anything passing the version check is cast to CodexExportV1_2<...>. A malformed codex JSON flows downstream as a typed-but-broken payload.
findings.evidence: codec.ts:83-93 — JSON.parse, check parsed is object, check version === '1.2', then `return parsed as CodexExportV1_2<...>` with no further shape check.
findings.impact: A user importing a corrupted backup file sees the failure at the next consumer (typically when the UI tries to render kadenaWallets[i].secret and crashes), not at the import boundary. Degrades recovery UX.
findings.suggestedFix: Add minimal shape check after version check: assert kadenaWallets/ouronetWallets/addressBook/uiSettings are arrays/objects of expected shape. Or pull in zod for full runtime validation.

findings.title: KeyResolver.requestForeignKey is optional but treated as recoverable
findings.description: KeyResolver.requestForeignKey method is optional. CodexSigningStrategy checks `this.resolver.requestForeignKey ? ... : undefined` and falls back to undefined. If the tx demands a foreign-key signer and the resolver doesn't implement the method, failure surfaces as universalSignTransaction complaining about a missing private key — opaque and several layers deep.
findings.evidence: codexStrategy.ts:180-182: `const onMissingKey = this.resolver.requestForeignKey ? (pub) => this.resolver.requestForeignKey!(pub) : undefined;` — silent fall-through.
findings.impact: Server-side resolvers (HUB) that intentionally omit requestForeignKey produce a confusing error path when a tx contains a foreign key. The contract doesn't say 'throwing here is the explicit signal' — it just lets the call proceed and fail downstream.
findings.suggestedFix: Make requestForeignKey required, document that server resolvers throw with a clear message. Or short-circuit in the strategy with a precise error before reaching universalSignTransaction.

findings.title: safeCreationTime is duplicated in 7 interactions/* files
findings.description: The same 3-line helper (`Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) - 30`) is copy-pasted into 7 files. The -30 value is the safety margin preventing Pact's 'creation time too far in the future' error. Changing it requires editing 7 files.
findings.evidence: grep 'function safeCreationTime' src/ returns 7 hits, all identical bodies.
findings.impact: Future drift between copies (e.g. one file uses -60 while six others stay at -30) is invisible until a chain rejection. The -30 is undocumented as a tuning knob.
findings.suggestedFix: Move to src/pact/index.ts (next to formatDecimalForPact). Update the 7 imports.

findings.title: classifyGuardKind discriminates on string presence, not value validity
findings.description: classifyGuardKind returns 'capability' if the object has a string-typed cgName field, regardless of whether the rest of the capability-guard shape (cgArgs, cgPactId) is present and valid. A malformed guard with { cgName: '', cgArgs: null } classifies as a capability and short-circuits further analysis.
findings.evidence: smartAccountAuth.ts:88: `if ('cgName' in o && typeof o.cgName === 'string') return 'capability';` — no check on cgArgs/cgPactId.
findings.impact: Malformed guards pass shape discrimination, are flagged 'not signable', and the user is misdirected to Execute Code page when the actual issue is a corrupt guard payload.
findings.suggestedFix: Tighten the discriminator: require the full minimal shape per kind, fall through to 'unknown' otherwise.

findings.title: extractKeysetFromGuard ignores the keysetRef post-resolution shape inconsistency
findings.description: extractKeysetFromGuard checks classifyGuardKind(g) !== 'keyset' and returns null. A keyset-ref resolved upstream (via resolveGuard) and now having { pred, keys, keysetRef } passes the keyset check — but the code preserves keysetRef only if o.keysetRef is truthy. The casing inconsistency (keysetref vs keysetRef) means a resolved ref's keysetRef field can land at the wrong key.
findings.evidence: smartAccountAuth.ts:120-124: type cast uses keysetRef (camelCase); the upstream classifier at :91 checks 'keysetref' (lowercase). Whichever resolver hydrates the field decides which casing wins.
findings.impact: Resolved keyset-refs may lose their keysetRef metadata silently, breaking later lookups by ref-name.
findings.suggestedFix: Normalize the case: pick one of keysetRef/keysetref and convert at the boundary (the upstream resolveGuard).

findings.title: analyzeSmartAccountAuthPaths state combinations not documented
findings.description: firstSatisfied === -1 AND anyKeyBased === true is a valid state: a key-based branch exists but no codex key signs it. Callers reading 'anyKeyBased = true' assume they can prompt for keys; without firstSatisfied, they may default to an unsignable branch and silently fail.
findings.evidence: smartAccountAuth.ts:229-237 — anyKeyBased and firstSatisfied are computed independently; no JSDoc explains the combination semantics.
findings.impact: UI may default-select an unsignable branch and silently fail when the user is required to first add a key.
findings.suggestedFix: Document state combinations in JSDoc. Consider adding firstSignableButUnsatisfied: number for the prompt-the-user path.

findings.title: Predicate fallback to keys-all on unknown predicates
findings.description: On an unknown predicate, computeThreshold returns keyCount (equivalent to keys-all) and writes a console.warn. Intent (fail closed) is correct, but: warning never propagates to caller; users with typo'd predicates get silent enforcement of keys-all and may lock themselves out; codex-side analysis diverges from on-chain truth.
findings.evidence: guardUtils.ts:76-79: `console.warn('[guardUtils] Unknown predicate:', pred, '— defaulting to keys-all'); return keyCount;`
findings.impact: UI shows 'needs N signatures' when real on-chain requirement is unknown. Codex side may report unsatisfiable when chain would accept fewer (or vice-versa).
findings.suggestedFix: Return a marker value or throw a typed UnknownPredicateError, so the analyzer can flag the guard as 'predicate not understood' rather than silently treating it as keys-all.

findings.title: README status table lags behind v1.6.x
findings.description: Recent commit 25c054d updates README from 1.3.0 → 1.4.0, but the codebase is at v1.6.1 with two more feature commits since then.
findings.evidence: git log --oneline shows v1.4.0, v1.5.0, v1.6.0, v1.6.1 commits; README still references 1.3.0/1.4.0 transition.
findings.impact: Consumers reading README to discover the public API miss Smart-account primitives entirely.
findings.suggestedFix: Update README to reflect v1.6.1 and add a v1.6.0 section listing the new Smart-account primitives.

findings.title: CONTEXT.md interactions section missing v1.4-v1.6 additions
findings.description: CONTEXT.md interactions section doesn't list v1.4 (AccountSelectorData public-key/sovereign/governor), v1.5 (createGen1Primitive), or v1.6 (Rotate Sovereign + Smart Account auth-path) additions.
findings.evidence: CONTEXT.md predates the four most recent feature commits.
findings.impact: Next agent or contributor reading CONTEXT.md to plan changes misses recent surface area.
findings.suggestedFix: Refresh CONTEXT.md interactions section to v1.6.1 surface.

findings.title: console.error inconsistency — sentinel-returning siblings vs ouroFunctions logging style
findings.description: Within ouroFunctions.ts, some catch blocks log (`} catch (error) { console.error(...); return null; }`) and others are silent (`} catch { return null; }`). No documented convention picks which.
findings.evidence: ouroFunctions.ts:1842, 1860, 1877, 1894 — silent catches. :1916, :2069, :2088 — logging catches. Same pattern within the same file.
findings.impact: Production observability is inconsistent — some failures land in console, others silently return null.
findings.suggestedFix: Pick one convention (log everything to a central logger, or log nothing) and apply consistently.

findings.title: tier argument is accepted-and-ignored by default reader
findings.description: The default reader ignores tier. Only OuronetUI's cache-aware reader honours it. New contributors don't know which tier is correct and copy whatever the neighbouring call uses.
findings.evidence: rawCalibratedRead.ts:40-46 documents tier as accepted-and-ignored: 'The raw read has no cache, so the tier is meaningless here'.
findings.impact: Tier mis-tagging on new code goes unnoticed until a HUB cache miss pattern emerges — by then the wrong tier is baked in across many call sites.
findings.suggestedFix: Document tier semantics in pactReader.ts JSDoc with the canonical mapping (T1=balance, T2=preview, T3=metadata, etc.). Add a runtime warning in dev mode if tier is missing.

findings.title: getLPTypeInfo outer try/catch is dead code
findings.description: Both inner async IIFEs in Promise.all already swallow errors into false. Promise.all cannot reject. The outer `catch (error) { return { hasFrozenLP: false, hasSleepingLP: false }; }` is unreachable.
findings.evidence: addLiquidityFunctions.ts:240-288 — inner try/catches at 256-258 and 274-276 both `return false`. Promise.all of two never-rejecting promises cannot itself reject.
findings.impact: Dead defensive code; harmless but confusing.
findings.suggestedFix: Drop the outer try/catch, or comment it as 'belt-and-braces in case someone removes the inner catches'.

findings.title: console.warn/error scattered across modules with no central logger
findings.description: No central logger; every module decides whether to emit and at what level. Consumers can't reroute (e.g. to Sentry) without monkey-patching console.
findings.evidence: grep -n 'console.error\|console.warn' src/ shows ~25 hits across 8 files.
findings.impact: Library produces unpaginated console noise in production. Consumers cannot filter, route, or suppress without monkey-patching.
findings.suggestedFix: Introduce src/observability/logger.ts with a setLogger(fn) injection point, mirror what setPactReader does. Replace console.error/warn with logger.error/warn.

needs_context.title: primitives.ts toHexString may have drifted from OuronetUI original
needs_context.blocker: Need read access to OuronetUI's src/lib/universalSign.ts to confirm whether toHexString is in the originals (the file header allows additions IF in originals).

needs_context.title: getLPTypeInfo Promise.all rejection wording vs intent
needs_context.blocker: Outer dead catch is confirmed (see F-CORE-021). Whether the dead catch is intentional defensive coding or copy-paste artifact depends on author intent.

needs_context.title: Inconsistent indentation across interactions/*
needs_context.blocker: Auditor cited no specific lines. Needs follow-up scan with a formatter (Prettier) to enumerate exact sites.

needs_context.title: cfm-builders test edge-case coverage
needs_context.blocker: Auditor flagged 'not enough lines reviewed to fully judge coverage gap'. Re-run testing-auditor with the test file as primary input to enumerate which builders have inputs but no negative-path test.

needs_context.title: getRotateKadenaInfo console.error inconsistency
needs_context.blocker: Pattern is consistent within ouroFunctions.ts; conflict only with sentinel-returning siblings elsewhere. Needs an explicit project-wide convention before this can be flagged definitively.

false_positives.title: getRandomValues().slice() pattern theoretical fragility
false_positives.reason: Currently safe (TextEncoder + WebCrypto contract holds); fragility is theoretical-only and not observable in any reachable code path.

false_positives.title: smartEncrypt claimed V2→V1 downgrade
false_positives.reason: Auditor misread documented intentional behaviour in tests/encryption-upgrade.test.ts:68-78. Dispatch is correct: schemaVersion >= 1 writes V2; lower writes V1.

false_positives.title: setTimeout orphan in isHealthy
false_positives.reason: clearTimeout(timeout) IS present at src/network/nodeFailover.ts:41, and the AbortController self-clears on response. No leak.

false_positives.title: src/index.ts empty
false_positives.reason: Intentional design (subpath-only consumer pattern). Documented at src/interactions/index.ts:1-14.

false_positives.title: strategy.test.ts order assertion
false_positives.reason: Auditor misread the file — assertion IS present at the cited lines.
